rationale: The cited paper appears only once, in the endogeneity/IV discussion, as an example supporting the claim that wholesale prices can serve as cost-shock instruments because consumers do not observe them. The citing paper does not build its theory, hypotheses, or core empirical framework on Chintagunta (2002), nor does it engage its specific findings about retailer category pricing objectives. This is a brief methodological precedent citation rather than a paper-specific dependency.
